_source: Extracted from platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/tool-use/overview and the Strict tool use docs. This is the actual schema format Anthropic requires for tool use with Claude.

client_tools_vs_server_tools.client_tools: Run in the developer's application. Includes user-defined tools and Anthropic-schema tools like bash and text_editor. Claude responds with stop_reason: 'tool_use' + tool_use blocks; your code executes and sends back tool_result.
client_tools_vs_server_tools.server_tools: Run on Anthropic's infrastructure. Examples: web_search, code_execution, web_fetch, tool_search. Results returned directly without developer handling execution. Reduces developer-side attack surface.

strict_tool_use.guarantee: Claude's tool calls will always match the schema exactly
strict_tool_use.documentation_quote: Add `strict: true` to your tool definitions to ensure Claude's tool calls always match your schema exactly.
strict_tool_use.mechanism: Constrains Claude's decoding to schema-conforming JSON, similar in principle to OpenAI's strict mode

tools_examples.name: get_weather
tools_examples.description: Get the current weather for a given location.

location.description: The city and state, e.g. San Francisco, CA

tools_examples.name: web_search
tools_examples._doc: Anthropic-hosted server tool. Runs in Anthropic infrastructure; results returned to model context as content blocks.
Sandboxed code execution on Anthropic infrastructure. The model can run Python in an isolated environment without the developer hosting a sandbox.
Fetch a URL through Anthropic's infrastructure (read-only).
Dynamically load relevant tool definitions at runtime for vendors with large tool catalogs. Reduces context tokens consumed by tool definitions.

tools_examples.name: issue_refund
tools_examples.description: Issue a refund against an order.
tools_examples._note: Like OpenAI, Anthropic's schema does not have first-class destructiveness or idempotency fields. These belong in the developer's tool implementation or in lifecycle hooks (PreToolUse / PostToolUse).

agent_sdk_built_in_tools.name: Read
agent_sdk_built_in_tools.description: Read any file in the working directory

agent_sdk_built_in_tools.name: Edit
agent_sdk_built_in_tools.description: Make precise edits to existing files

agent_sdk_built_in_tools.name: Bash
agent_sdk_built_in_tools.description: Run terminal commands, scripts, git operations

agent_sdk_built_in_tools.name: Monitor
agent_sdk_built_in_tools.description: Watch a background script and react to each output line as an event

agent_sdk_built_in_tools.name: WebSearch
agent_sdk_built_in_tools.description: Search the web for current information

agent_sdk_built_in_tools.name: WebFetch
agent_sdk_built_in_tools.description: Fetch and parse web page content

agent_sdk_built_in_tools.name: AskUserQuestion
agent_sdk_built_in_tools.description: Ask the user clarifying questions with multiple choice options

agent_sdk_built_in_tools.name: Agent
agent_sdk_built_in_tools.description: Invoke a subagent for delegated work

tool_choice._doc: Hard guarantee about which tools the model can use. Default 'auto' lets Claude decide; 'any' forces some tool; 'tool' with a name forces that specific tool.

permission_modes.default: Prompts the user (or hook) for approval on risky tool use
permission_modes.acceptEdits: Auto-approves file edits; useful for trusted CI flows
permission_modes.bypassPermissions: Runs without prompting — only use in trusted environments
permission_modes.plan: Shows what the agent would do without executing; equivalent to dry-run

validation_layer.strategy: Strict tool use constrains Claude's decoding to schema-conforming JSON. Schema-invalid output is structurally impossible.
validation_layer._developer_pattern: Use PreToolUse hooks to add foreign-key checks, business rules, or approval gates before tool execution. The hooks framework is finer-grained than guardrails-in-parallel.
